place: Heislerville CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Heislerville got its name from the Heisler family, prominent Methodist Episcopal Church members who settled there in 1828.
A survey marker east of Milmay, placed by the 1688 West New Jersey Proprietors, once divided three townships.
The Maurice River Historical Society, founded in 1980, preserves Heislerville’s maritime and agricultural heritage in its museum and research library.
Heislerville’s railroad station, “Link City,” once shipped thousands of strawberries and supported nearby oyster packing operations.
The town’s museum showcases Native American artifacts, maritime history, and local industry, reflecting the region’s diverse past.
In 1995, the town’s historic bridge was added to the National Register of Historic Places for its engineering and transportation significance.
